1	import type { PageLoad } from './$types'
2	import { createClient } from '$lib/gql/client'
3	import { gql } from 'graphql-request'
4	
5	const DOC_GRAPH_QUERY = gql`
6		query DocGraph {
7			docGraph {
8				rootDir
9				documents {
10					id
11					path
12					title
13					dependsOn
14					dependents
15				}
16				pathToId
17				warnings
18				issues {
19					issueId
20					kind
21					path
22					id
23					message
24					relatedPath
25				}
26			}
27		}
28	`
29	
30	const DOC_STALE_QUERY = gql`
31		query DocStale {
32			docStale {
33				id
34			}
35		}
36	`
37	
38	export interface GraphDocument {
39		id: string
40		path: string
41		title: string
42		dependsOn: string[]
43		dependents: string[]
44		staleness: string
45		mediaType: string
46	}
47	
48	export interface GraphIssue {
49		issueId: string
50		kind: string
51		path: string | null
52		id: string | null
53		message: string
54		relatedPath: string | null
55	}
56	
57	interface RawGraphDocument {
58		id: string
59		path: string
60		title: string
61		dependsOn: string[]
62		dependents: string[]
63	}
64	
65	interface DocGraph {
66		rootDir: string
67		documents: RawGraphDocument[]
68		pathToId: string
69		warnings: string[]
70		issues: GraphIssue[]
71	}
72	
73	interface DocGraphResult {
74		docGraph: DocGraph
75	}
76	
77	interface StaleResult {
78		docStale: Array<{ id: string }>
79	}
80	
81	function mediaTypeFromPath(path: string): string {
82		const ext = path.split('.').pop()?.toLowerCase() ?? ''
83		if (ext === 'md') return 'text/markdown'
84		if (['png', 'jpg', 'jpeg', 'gif', 'webp'].includes(ext)) return 'image/*'
85		if (ext === 'svg') return 'image/svg+xml'
86		return 'unknown'
87	}
88	
89	export const load: PageLoad = async ({ fetch, url }) => {
90		const client = createClient(fetch as unknown as typeof globalThis.fetch)
91	
92		const [graphData, staleData] = await Promise.all([
93			client.request<DocGraphResult>(DOC_GRAPH_QUERY),
94			client
95				.request<StaleResult>(DOC_STALE_QUERY)
96				.catch(() => ({ docStale: [] as Array<{ id: string }> }))
97		])
98	
99		const graph = graphData.docGraph
100		const staleIds = new Set(staleData.docStale.map((s) => s.id))
101	
102		let pathToDocId: Record<string, string> = {}
103		try {
104			pathToDocId = JSON.parse(graph.pathToId ?? '{}')
105		} catch {
106			pathToDocId = {}
107		}
108	
109		const documents: GraphDocument[] = graph.documents.map((doc) => ({
110			...doc,
111			staleness: staleIds.has(doc.id) ? 'stale' : 'fresh',
112			mediaType: mediaTypeFromPath(doc.path)
113		}))
114	
115		// Parse initial viewport from URL params
116		const zoomParam = parseFloat(url.searchParams.get('zoom') ?? '')
117		const panParam = url.searchParams.get('pan')
118		const panParts = panParam ? panParam.split(',').map(parseFloat) : null
119		const initialTransform =
120			!isNaN(zoomParam) && panParts && panParts.length === 2 && panParts.every((v) => !isNaN(v))
121				? { x: panParts[0], y: panParts[1], k: zoomParam }
122				: null
123	
124		const highlightNodeId = url.searchParams.get('highlight') ?? undefined
125	
126		return {
127			graph: {
128				...graph,
129				documents,
130				pathToDocId,
131				issues: graph.issues ?? []
132			},
133			initialTransform,
134			highlightNodeId
135		}
136	}

git -C /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-7140a0cf-20260502T123239-237d0c38 commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(web-ui): keep graph edges visible in dark mode [ddx-7140a0cf]

Replace the border-line tokens + 0.6 stroke-opacity multiplier (~1.1:1
contrast in dark mode) with graph-local --graph-edge / --graph-edge-arrow
CSS vars at #766F65 / #9C9486. Subtlety now comes from color, not alpha,
so edges and arrowheads clear the WCAG 3:1 non-text floor on the warm
canvas in both themes.

Adds a Playwright dark-mode test (TC-042) that reads the painted stroke
and arrow fill via getComputedStyle, alpha-composites them onto the
canvas (catching any regressed opacity), and asserts >=3:1. The fixture
renders fresh / stale / missing nodes in one frame so the contrast check
exercises every staleness state. Page loader passes a doc.staleness
through when supplied so the fixture can drive the missing state.

Pre-existing TC-040 failure ("element is outside of the viewport" with
preset zoom/pan) reproduces on the base commit and is unrelated.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

data.text:
Committed cfe6baba. Summary:

- **`src/app.css`**: Added `--graph-edge` / `--graph-edge-arrow` CSS vars (`#766F65` light, `#9C9486` dark) plus `.graph-edge` / `.graph-edge-arrow` utility classes.
- **`D3Graph.svelte`**: Switched edge `<line>` and arrow `<path>` to the new classes; dropped the `stroke-opacity 0.6` multiplier.
- **`graph/+page.ts`**: Pass-through optional `staleness` so a fixture can drive the `missing` state.
- **`D3Graph.contrast.spec.ts`**: Now reads `--graph-edge` from `app.css` and runs the contrast helper (no token-string equality).
- **`graph.spec.ts`**: New `TC-042` mounts a fresh/stale/missing fixture in dark mode, alpha-composites edge stroke and arrow fill onto the painted canvas color, and asserts ≥3:1.

Verified: `bun run test:unit -- --run D3Graph.contrast.spec.ts` (3/3 pass), `bun run test:e2e -- graph -g TC-042` passes, full graph suite passes 12/13. The single failure (TC-040) reproduces on the base commit (`?zoom=2.000&pan=100.0,50.0` lands the click target outside the viewport) — pre-existing, unrelated to this bead.
